Article Title: LeBron James’ son Bronny James drops promising stats amid absence from the main Lakers’ roster
Article Text:
Bronny James. Image via: Nic Coury/ AP

Bronny James drops remarkable G-League stats



Bronny James receives ultimate one-on-one challenge from boxer



It’s hard to ignore Bronny James. The son of legendary (arguably the greatest of all time) LeBron James , Bronny made his NBA debut on October 22, 2024, and played alongside his father in a historic move. Still, Bronny has not been a regular in the main roster as the Lakers' front office often sends him to their G-League affiliate South Bay Lakers. Still, no complaints. Bronny is making sure that he keeps his name relevant in the basketball scene.Bronny recently logged five minutes in the Lakers’ 128-117 victory over the Indiana Pacers. The outing did not produce counting statistics, which reflects the limited role he currently holds under head coach JJ Redick.Soon after the game, the organization assigned him back to South Bay. The move allowed him to receive extended playing time and continue his on-court development while competing in a more consistent role.Bronny James delivered a solid performance in South Bay’s recent 113-104 victory over the Santa Cruz Warriors. Starting in the matchup, the 6-foot-2 guard finished with 15 points, two assists, and one rebound in 25 minutes.One moment quickly caught attention when he attacked the basket for a powerful poster dunk during the second quarter, pushing the South Bay lead to 62-47 late in the period.The South Bay Lakers controlled the game after a dominant second quarter in which they scored 43 points to build a 67–52 halftime advantage. The team shot 46.7 percent from the field and knocked down 18 three-pointers, creating enough separation to maintain control even after Santa Cruz made a late push.Bronny’s performance added to a growing set of productive G League outings. Across his appearances with South Bay this season, he has averaged around 15 points, along with close to four assists and over three rebounds per game.While Bronny’s performances in the G League continue to attract attention, an unexpected moment away from the court recently generated conversation across the sports world. During an episode of the podcast 7PM in Brooklyn, hosted by Carmelo Anthony, boxing champion Shakur Stevenson discussed the idea of competing against the young guard.The topic surfaced when podcast co-host Kazim Famuyide mentioned a potential one-on-one matchup between Stevenson and Bronny. Stevenson responded with enthusiasm, saying, "Set it up. I got Bronny."Anthony quickly added excitement to the conversation by saying, "Blackout runs this summer coming."The exchange quickly circulated online and sparked discussions among fans, even though it came across as a playful idea rather than a serious cross-sport challenge.
Article Summary: Bronny James made his NBA debut on October 22, 2024, and played alongside his father in a historic move. Still, Bronny has not been a regular in the main roster as the Lakers' front office often sends him to their G-League affiliate South Bay Lakers. Bronny delivered a solid performance in South Bay’s recent 113-104 victory over the Santa Cruz Warriors.

Article Title: “I missed you girl”: Jayson Tatum posts emotional reaction after game-sealing dagger following long-awaited Celtics return
Article Text:
Jayson Tatum. Image via: Nathaniel S. Butler, NBAE via Getty Images/AFP

Jayson Tatum celebrates Celtics return with clutch dagger and emotional message



Jayson Tatum addresses his Celtics return



The Boston Celtics received a major boost this week as star forward Jayson Tatum continued his return to the lineup following a long injury recovery. The six-time All-Star played a huge role in Boston’s 109–98 road win over the Cleveland Cavaliers on Sunday, delivering a late three-pointer that helped seal the result. Soon after the game, Tatum shared a heartfelt message on social media that quickly caught fans’ attention.The post was a clear reflection of the emotional side of his comeback after months away from the court. Tatum had missed a large stretch following a ruptured right Achilles tendon during the 2025 playoffs. His return brought excitement across the Celtics community, and the latest performance showed signs that he is settling back into rhythm with his teammates during a crucial stretch of the season.Boston controlled most of the matchup in Cleveland. Jaylen Brown produced a strong all-around performance with 23 points, nine rebounds, and eight assists as the Celtics built a large lead. Jayson Tatum added 20 points on 6-of-16 shooting in his second game back in the lineup.Late in the fourth quarter, as Boston was protecting its advantage, Tatum knocked down a three-pointer with 1:59 remaining. The basket extended the lead and helped close the door on Cleveland’s comeback attempt.After the victory, Tatum took to Instagram and posted an emotional message: “I missed you girl.”The Celtics forward had already shown glimpses of his impact earlier in the game. He scored 12 points in a quick burst during the opening quarter to help Boston grab control early.Tatum later reflected on his comeback and the feeling of returning to a normal game routine. Speaking after the win, he described how the second game of his return felt more familiar compared to the emotional home debut earlier in the week.“Today felt a lot more normal, just from a preparation standpoint in this league,” he said. “It felt like being back in the flow again.“It’s just amazing how happy I am to be back and wanting to be out there more.”Tatum’s return was also personal as his mother, Brandy Cole-Barnes, made a surprise trip to Cleveland to watch him play. The two remained close throughout the lengthy rehabilitation process that followed surgery last May.“She texted me this morning to say, ‘Hey, I’m coming to Cleveland,’” he said, smiling. “I looked up in the crowd and she was just crying. Seeing me on the court obviously brings her a lot of joy.”Boston is now managing Tatum’s minutes as he works back to full strength. He logged 27 minutes in the game and played key stretches alongside Brown and Payton Pritchard.
Article Summary: Jayson Tatum scored 20 points on 6-of-16 shooting in his second game back. Tatum missed a large stretch following a ruptured right Achilles tendon during the 2025 playoffs. The Celtics beat the Cleveland Cavaliers 109–98 on Sunday.

Article Title: Tyrese Maxey and girlfriend Myra Gordon drops heartfelt personal update amid NBA injury
Article Text:
Tyrese Maxey and girlfriend Myra. Image via: Sarah Stier/ Getty Images

Tyrese Maxey and girlfriend Myra Gordon announce personal news



Tyrese Maxey and Myra Gordon. Image via: myra.christina/ Instagram

A brief look at Tyrese Maxey and girlfriend Myra Gordon relationship



Life away from the court brought meaningful news for Tyrese Maxey during a challenging week. The Philadelphia 76ers guard is currently sidelined after suffering a sprained right fifth finger. The injury forced him to miss upcoming games while doctors evaluate the next steps in his recovery. The update arrived after Maxey injured his hand late in a loss to the Atlanta Hawks, which left the All-Star guard unavailable for Philadelphia’s back-to-back matchups against the Cleveland Cavaliers and Memphis Grizzlies.But there is some good news as well. Maxey and his girlfriend, Myra Gordon, shared personal news with fans through social media.While Philadelphia continues its push in a tight Eastern Conference playoff race, Maxey’s personal milestone quickly drew interest across the basketball world.Myra revealed that she and Maxey are expecting their first child together. The news surfaced through an Instagram post that featured a series of photos of the couple celebrating the moment.She wrote, "i carry her while he carries us 🫶🏽." Maxey responded in the comments with a short message of his own, writing "MM 🫶🏾" while also sharing one of the images from the post on his Instagram story.The news followed a previous message Myra shared on her 24th birthday while reflecting on the journey that led to the pregnancy announcement. In that earlier post, she wrote, "Year 24. Beyond blessed for every trial, tribulation, and obstacle this past year brought my way — each one shaped me into the strongest version of myself yet. I know now it was all preparing me for this next chapter,"She continued with a message about the upcoming arrival."To my friends and family who’ve stood by me through it all, I can’t put into words how much I love and appreciate you. Baby M, your mommy is so excited to meet you. You’re already surrounded by more love than you could ever imagine. 🫶🏽."Gordon, a college basketball standout, entered the spotlight earlier through her previous relationship with Bryce Young, the quarterback for the Carolina Panthers. Reports indicated that the pair parted ways in 2024.Maxey later confirmed he was dating Gordon earlier this year. The relationship gained public attention as Gordon occasionally appeared around the NBA environment. She was seen courtside during a January game between the 76ers and the Orlando Magic, which further fueled discussion among fans following the couple’s social media activity.
Article Summary: Philadelphia 76ers guard Tyrese Maxey is currently sidelined with a sprained finger. Maxey and his girlfriend, Myra Gordon, shared personal news with fans through social media. Myra revealed that she and Maxey are expecting their first child together.

Article Title: Los Angeles Lakers claim huge victory over New York Knicks in star-studded night as Martin Lawrence and several other celebrities cheered with crowd
Article Text:
Austin Reaves and Karl-Anthony Towns. Image via: Caroline Brehman/ AP

Celebrities attend the Lakers-Knicks game



Lakers secure huge win over Knicks



The Los Angeles Lakers delivered one of their strongest performances of the season Sunday afternoon, defeating the New York Knicks 110–97 at Crypto.com Arena. The matchup between two of the NBA ’s most historic franchises carried a big-game feel from the start. Fans packed the arena, and the crowd reflected the cultural pull of both cities. New York supporters arrived wearing Knicks gear, while Los Angeles fans filled the seats with purple and gold.The contest also turned into a celebrity gathering that mirrored a Hollywood premiere. Several well-known figures sat courtside as the Lakers controlled the game from the opening quarter. Actor Martin Lawrence watched closely from a seat near the Lakers' bench while the home team built momentum.Even without LeBron James in uniform, Los Angeles maintained control in the game.The atmosphere inside the arena stretched beyond basketball. The Lakers and Knicks often draw high-profile crowds, and Sunday’s matchup continued that trend. Martin Lawrence enjoyed the action courtside, seated across from James’ usual spot near the bench. Internet personality Jordan Howlett, known online as “Jordan the Stallion,” sat nearby.Queens-born rapper Action Bronson, a longtime Knicks supporter, followed the game while wearing a Knicks hat. Across the court, comedian and actor Sacha Baron Cohen observed the matchup quietly.Several other notable figures joined the audience. Television host Bill Maher attended with film producer Noor Alfallah. Actor Andy Garcia also sat courtside. Knicks legend Patrick Ewing and former Lakers center Dwight Howard were present as well.Musician Flea of the Red Hot Chili Peppers attended with designer Melody Ehsani and their son. Sports agent Rich Paul watched from his regular courtside seat. Comedian Jay Mohr sat beside Lakers governor Jeanie Buss. Actor Ray Nicholson, son of longtime Lakers fan Jack Nicholson, occupied his father’s well-known courtside seat. Larry David, Timothée Chalamet, Spike Lee, Ben Stiller, Kylie Jenner, Denzel Washington, John McEnroe, Brenda Song, Macaulay Culkin, Jason Sudeikis, Kevin Connolly, Kyler Murray, and NFL star Puka Nacua also attended the night.On the court, Los Angeles set the tone early. Luka Dončić led the offense with 35 points, eight rebounds, four assists, and two steals. His scoring burst in the third quarter helped stretch the Lakers’ lead and shifted the momentum firmly in their favor.Austin Reaves contributed 25 points along with four rebounds and five assists. Rui Hachimura added 13 points and seven rebounds. Luke Kennard scored 12 points off the bench.Karl-Anthony Towns finished with 25 points and 16 rebounds for the Knicks, and Jalen Brunson added 24 points and seven assists. The Knicks struggled with turnovers and outside shooting, which slowed their comeback effort late in the game.Despite a brief fourth-quarter push from New York, Los Angeles closed the final minutes with key baskets to secure the 13-point win.
Article Summary: The Los Angeles Lakers defeated the New York Knicks 110–97 at Crypto.com Arena. Actor Martin Lawrence watched closely from a seat near the Lakers' bench. Larry David, Timothée Chalamet, Spike Lee, Ben Stiller, Kylie Jenner, Denzel Washington, John McEnroe, Brenda Song, Macaulay Culkin, Jason Sudeikis, Kyler Murray, and NFL star Puka Nacua also attended.

Article Title: NBA injury update: Philadelphia 76ers hit with another unfortunate news amid Joel Embiid and Paul George’s absence
Article Text:
Tyrese Maxey. Image via: Dale Zanine-Imagn Images

All-Star guard Tyrese Maxey

What happened to Tyrese Maxey?



Philadelphia 76ers' trouble extends as Joel Embiid and Paul George are out



The Philadelphia 76ers face another setback at a critical stage of the NBA season. As the regular season is winding down and playoff positioning tightens in the Eastern Conference, the team received difficult news regarding. The injury update arrives at a time when Philadelphia is already reeling through the absence of its biggest names.Philadelphia entered the week holding the No. 8 seed in the East with a 34-29 record. The margin in the standings remains slim, with several teams packed closely together in the race to avoid the play-in tournament. Losing a key contributor, even for a short stretch, creates a new challenge for a roster that has already dealt with multiple disruptions this season.Tyrese Maxey has been the most dependable player for Philadelphia this season. The guard has appeared in 61 games, while teammates Joel Embiid and Paul George have combined for 60 appearances. His role has been very important for the team throughout the year, and he earned an All-Star starting spot in the Eastern Conference after delivering consistent production on both ends of the floor.The 25-year-old suffered a sprained right fifth finger during Saturday’s loss to the Atlanta Hawks. The injury occurred in the final seconds of the game when Maxey collided with teammate Adem Bona while diving for a loose ball with roughly 16 seconds remaining. He immediately grabbed his right hand in visible pain before heading to the locker room.Philadelphia later confirmed the injury in an official statement.“Tyrese Maxey suffered a sprain of the right fifth finger. He will undergo additional testing and consultation in the coming days to determine a treatment plan. He is OUT for the next two games and further updates will be provided after the back-to-back.”Maxey will miss the upcoming games against the Cleveland Cavaliers and the Memphis Grizzlies. The guard sprained the same finger last season, which affected his shooting rhythm after returning. This season he has averaged 29 points and 6.7 assists while shooting 37.3 percent from three-point range. When he leaves the floor, Philadelphia is outscored by 4.6 points per 100 possessions, which highlights his value to the offense.Maxey’s absence adds to an already difficult situation for the 76ers. Embiid remains sidelined with a right oblique strain that he sustained during a game against the Miami Heat on Feb. 26. He has already missed several games and is expected to remain out for additional time.George is also unavailable. The veteran forward is serving a 25-game suspension after violating the NBA’s anti-drug policy and will not be eligible to return until March 25.The 76ers hope Maxey’s injury proves minor. The team expects further medical evaluations in the coming days before deciding the next step in his recovery timeline.
Article Summary: Tyrese Maxey suffered a sprained right fifth finger during Saturday's loss to the Atlanta Hawks. The 25-year-old will miss the upcoming games against the Cleveland Cavaliers and the Memphis Grizzlies. Maxey has been the most dependable player for the 76ers this season.

Both teams are trying to strengthen their position in the standings as the season moves closer to the postseason.LaMelo Ball dealt with ankle tendinopathy earlier in the season. The issue limited him to 22 games during the early part of the year.He has since returned and is playing regular minutes again. There was a brief concern when he left a game on March 4, but it did not lead to a longer absence.In Charlotte’s March 6 game against Miami, Ball scored 21 points while shooting 7-for-22 from the field and 5-for-15 from three-point range.
